_metadata.source: Arlington County assessment data and market observations
notes: Configurable market parameters for estimating development rights value.
All dollar values are illustrative defaults and must be calibrated to
current market conditions before use in policy analysis.
Parameter source: Arlington County residential land market observations.
This is NOT a substitute for a formal appraisal.

market_to_assessment_ratio.notes: Arlington assessments track close to market value. Range accounts for assessment lag and parcel-level variation.

price_per_available_gfa_sf.notes: Dollars per square foot of unused GFA capacity. Varies by neighborhood, zoning district, and current market conditions. Calibrate from comparable land sales expressed per buildable SF.

price_per_available_dwelling_unit.notes: Dollars per available (unused) dwelling unit of capacity. Reflects land component only, not construction cost. Most applicable for vacant lots where max_dwelling_units > 0.

land_residual_discount_factor.notes: Discount applied to the raw land-per-SF rate to account for: entitlement risk, demolition costs (if parcel is not vacant), holding and carry costs, and developer profit margin. Lower bound is more conservative.

confidence_thresholds.notes: Minimum values for 'HIGH' confidence rating. Below these thresholds, assessed land value or available GFA is too small to support a reliable estimate.

residential_improvement_value_per_sf.notes: Fallback assessed improvement value per SF used when the neighborhood-derived rate has insufficient sample. The pipeline now automatically derives this rate from homes built in the last N years (see neighborhood_rate_calibration). This fallback applies only when fewer than min_sample_size recent builds exist.

neighborhood_rate_calibration.notes: Parameters for deriving neighborhood-specific improvement $/SF from recent construction. Recent homes are assumed to be built at the specified fraction of the max building envelope. The rate is: improvement_value / (max_footprint * 2.5 stories * utilization). Median and +/- 1 std dev define the neighborhood range.
